attempts.response:
```spthy
theory CertificationPath_Token_P
begin

builtins: asymmetric-encryption, signing, hashing

functions:
  id/1   // abstract identity binding to a public key

/* Roles:
   - CA: Root certification authority that signs certificates binding identities to public keys.
   - A  (Sender/Originator): Registers for a certificate, then sends a signed token to B containing:
        tA (timestamp), rA (fresh nonce with sequential part), B's identity, non-confidential sgnData,
        and confidential encData encrypted under B's public key.
   - B  (Recipient/Verifier): Publishes its public key and identity, receives A's message, retrieves A's public key
        via the CA-signed certificate, verifies signature and fields, decrypts confidential data, and accepts.

   Message (A -> B):
     < certA = <<'cert', idA, pkA>, sigCA>,
       payload = <'TA', tA, 'RA', rA, 'B', idB, 'SGN', sgnData, 'ED', aenc(< 'kconf', kconf >, pkB)>,
       sig = sign(h(payload), skA) >

   Security goals:
     - Confidentiality of kconf (encData) against the adversary unless B's private key is compromised.
     - Origin authentication (non-injective agreement): If B accepts a token, then either A sent it or
       A's signing key was compromised.
*/

let CA(~skCA) =
  // Publish the CA's public key and issue certificates on request
  out(pk(~skCA));
  !(
    in(<'Reg', idA, pkA>);                            // A's registration request
    let cm   = <'cert', idA, pkA> in
    let sigC = sign(cm, ~skCA) in                     // simple cert: signature over the binding
    event CACert(idA, pkA);
    out(<cm, sigC>)                                   // certA = <<'cert', idA, pkA>, sigC>
  )

let A(~skA, pkCA) =
  // Register and obtain a certificate for pkA from the CA, then send a signed token to B
  let pkA = pk(~skA) in
  let idA = id(pkA) in
  out(<'Reg', idA, pkA>);
  in(certA);
  let <<'cert', =idA, =pkA>, sigCA> = certA in
  if verify(sigCA, <'cert', idA, pkA>, pkCA) = true then
    // Discover B's public key and identity (published by B)
    in(<'Peer', idB, pkB>);
    // Construct token
    new tA;                 // timestamp/freshness indicator
    new rA;                 // fresh, non-repeating number (with an implied sequential part)
    new sgnData;            // non-confidential parameters (visible in clear)
    new kconf;              // confidential parameter (e.g., session key or secret)
    let encData = aenc(<'kconf', kconf>, pkB) in
    let payload = <'TA', tA, 'RA', rA, 'B', idB, 'SGN', sgnData, 'ED', encData> in
    let sig     = sign(h(payload), ~skA) in            // X{Info} = Info, Xs[h(Info)]
    event TokenSent(pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, kconf);
    event Secret(kconf);
    out(<certA, payload, sig>)

let B(~skB, pkCA) =
  // Publish identity and public key; receive, verify and accept A's token
  let pkB = pk(~skB) in
  let idB = id(pkB) in
  out(<'Peer', idB, pkB>);                            // available for senders to target this recipient
  in(msg);
  let <certA, payload, sig> = msg in
  let <<'cert', idA, pkA>, sigCA> = certA in
  if verify(sigCA, <'cert', idA, pkA>, pkCA) = true then
    let <'TA', tA, 'RA', rA, 'B', idB2, 'SGN', sData, 'ED', encData> = payload in
    if idB2 = idB then                                 // intended recipient check
      if verify(sig, h(payload), pkA) = true then       // signature verification on hashed payload
        let <'kconf', kconf> = adec(encData, ~skB) in   // decrypt confidential parameters
        // Timestamp and anti-replay checks (abstracted here as comments; could be modeled with events/guards):
        // - Check "current" tA
        // - Optionally log/store <tA, rA> for uniqueness within validity window
        event Accept(pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sData, kconf);
        event ReceivedConfidential(kconf)

let LtkRevealProc(s) =
  // Long-term key compromise (used to condition lemmas)
  event LtkReveal(pk(s)); out(s)

process:
new ~skCA;
(
    !CA(~skCA)
  | !(
        new ~skA; out(pk(~skA));
        ( !A(~skA, pk(~skCA)) | LtkRevealProc(~skA) )
    )
  | !(
        new ~skB; out(pk(~skB));
        ( !B(~skB, pk(~skCA)) | LtkRevealProc(~skB) )
    )
)

lemma executable:
  exists-trace
  "Ex pkA pkB t r sd k #i.
     Accept(pkA, pkB, t, r, sd, k) @ #i"

lemma confidentiality_encData:
  "All pkA pkB t r sd k #i.
     Accept(pkA, pkB, t, r, sd, k) @ #i
     ==> not (Ex #j. K(k) @ #j)
         | (Ex #rB. LtkReveal(pkB) @ #rB)
  "

lemma origin_authentication_noninj:
  "All pkA pkB t r sd k #i.
     Accept(pkA, pkB, t, r, sd, k) @ #i
     ==> (Ex #j. TokenSent(pkA, pkB, t, r, sd, k) @ #j & #j < #i)
         | (Ex #rA. LtkReveal(pkA) @ #rA)
  "

end
```

Notes:
- The certification path B -> A is abstracted as a root CA certificate over A’s identity and public key; B verifies it using the CA’s public key.
- The token matches X{Info} = Info, Xs[h(Info)] by signing the hash of the payload and appending the signature.

theory CertificationPath_Token_P
begin

builtins: asymmetric-encryption, signing, hashing

functions:
  id/1   // abstract identity binding to a public key

/* Roles:
   - CA: Root certification authority that signs certificates binding identities to public keys.
   - A  (Sender/Originator): Registers for a certificate, then sends a signed token to B containing:
        tA (timestamp), rA (fresh nonce with sequential part), B's identity, non-confidential sgnData,
        and confidential encData encrypted under B's public key.
   - B  (Recipient/Verifier): Publishes its public key and identity, receives A's message, retrieves A's public key
        via the CA-signed certificate, verifies signature and fields, decrypts confidential data, and accepts.

   Message (A -> B):
     < certA = <<'cert', idA, pkA>, sigCA>,
       payload = <'TA', tA, 'RA', rA, 'B', idB, 'SGN', sgnData, 'ED', aenc(< 'kconf', kconf >, pkB)>,
       sig = sign(h(payload), skA) >

   Security goals:
     - Confidentiality of kconf (encData) against the adversary unless B's private key is compromised.
     - Origin authentication (non-injective agreement): If B accepts a token, then either A sent it or
       A's signing key was compromised.
*/

let CA(~skCA) =
  // Publish the CA's public key and issue certificates on request
  out(pk(~skCA));
  !(
    in(<'Reg', idA, pkA>);                            // A's registration request
    let cm   = <'cert', idA, pkA> in
    let sigC = sign(cm, ~skCA) in                     // simple cert: signature over the binding
    event CACert(idA, pkA);
    out(<cm, sigC>)                                   // certA = <<'cert', idA, pkA>, sigC>
  )

let A(~skA, pkCA) =
  // Register and obtain a certificate for pkA from the CA, then send a signed token to B
  let pkA = pk(~skA) in
  let idA = id(pkA) in
  out(<'Reg', idA, pkA>);
  in(certA);
  let <<'cert', =idA, =pkA>, sigCA> = certA in
  if verify(sigCA, <'cert', idA, pkA>, pkCA) = true then
    // Discover B's public key and identity (published by B)
    in(<'Peer', idB, pkB>);
    // Construct token
    new tA;                 // timestamp/freshness indicator
    new rA;                 // fresh, non-repeating number (with an implied sequential part)
    new sgnData;            // non-confidential parameters (visible in clear)
    new kconf;              // confidential parameter (e.g., session key or secret)
    let encData = aenc(<'kconf', kconf>, pkB) in
    let payload = <'TA', tA, 'RA', rA, 'B', idB, 'SGN', sgnData, 'ED', encData> in
    let sig     = sign(h(payload), ~skA) in            // X{Info} = Info, Xs[h(Info)]
    event TokenSent(pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, kconf);
    event Secret(kconf);
    out(<certA, payload, sig>)

let B(~skB, pkCA) =
  // Publish identity and public key; receive, verify and accept A's token
  let pkB = pk(~skB) in
  let idB = id(pkB) in
  out(<'Peer', idB, pkB>);                            // available for senders to target this recipient
  in(msg);
  let <certA, payload, sig> = msg in
  let <<'cert', idA, pkA>, sigCA> = certA in
  if verify(sigCA, <'cert', idA, pkA>, pkCA) = true then
    let <'TA', tA, 'RA', rA, 'B', idB2, 'SGN', sData, 'ED', encData> = payload in
    if idB2 = idB then                                 // intended recipient check
      if verify(sig, h(payload), pkA) = true then       // signature verification on hashed payload
        let <'kconf', kconf> = adec(encData, ~skB) in   // decrypt confidential parameters
        // Timestamp and anti-replay checks (abstracted here as comments; could be modeled with events/guards):
        // - Check "current" tA
        // - Optionally log/store <tA, rA> for uniqueness within validity window
        event Accept(pkA, pkB, tA, rA, sData, kconf);
        event ReceivedConfidential(kconf)

let LtkRevealProc(s) =
  // Long-term key compromise (used to condition lemmas)
  event LtkReveal(pk(s)); out(s)

process:
new ~skCA;
(
    !CA(~skCA)
  | !(
        new ~skA; out(pk(~skA));
        ( !A(~skA, pk(~skCA)) | LtkRevealProc(~skA) )
    )
  | !(
        new ~skB; out(pk(~skB));
        ( !B(~skB, pk(~skCA)) | LtkRevealProc(~skB) )
    )
)

lemma executable:
  exists-trace
  "Ex pkA pkB t r sd k #i.
     Accept(pkA, pkB, t, r, sd, k) @ #i"

lemma confidentiality_encData:
  "All pkA pkB t r sd k #i.
     Accept(pkA, pkB, t, r, sd, k) @ #i
     ==> not (Ex #j. K(k) @ #j)
         | (Ex #rB. LtkReveal(pkB) @ #rB)
  "

  "All pkA pkB t r sd k #i.
     Accept(pkA, pkB, t, r, sd, k) @ #i
     ==> (Ex #j. TokenSent(pkA, pkB, t, r, sd, k) @ #j & #j < #i)
         | (Ex #rA. LtkReveal(pkA) @ #rA)
